name: Rich Coconut Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Bake the coconut in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until tender.
3. Remove the baked coconut from the oven and use a hammer to crack it open into 4-5 pieces.
4. Discard the coconut water that drains away; it is not used.
5. Use a dull knife to carefully remove the coconut meat from the shell, avoiding any cuts to your fingers.
6. Cut the meat into strips about 1 inch wide, then slice horizontally into thin slices.
7. Place approximately 3 cups of the sliced coconut meat into a blender.
8. Add 4 cups of very hot water (using less water will result in a richer milk).
9. Blend on high for about 1 minute to break up the coconut and release its milk.
10. Pour the mixture through a metal sieve, pressing down gently to extract as much liquid as possible from the coconut pieces.
11. The extracted liquid is coconut milk; it can be stored refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen in plastic containers for later use.
